steps.title: Identify the independent clause structure
steps.content:
The sentence has one main verb: ensured. The subject is “A plano-convex lens,” followed by a phrase “the laser on the center of the 10-millimeter-long crystal,” and then the main verb.

The blank must be filled with a form that describes the lens's action without creating a second independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule
steps.content:
Choice C uses focusing, a present participle.

This creates a participial phrase that modifies the subject: “A plano-convex lens focusing the laser on the center of the 10-millimeter-long crystal” becomes the complete subject, and “ensured” stands as the single main verb of the sentence.

Choices A, B, and D use finite verb forms that would create two independent verbs in the same clause without proper coordination.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
